title: Shepherd's pie – simple, comforting home cooking
excerpt: Shepherd's pie is that food that doesn't have much prestige. But it is honest food. Food that doesn't ask questions and just settles gently in your stomach.

seo.title: Shepherd's pie - simple, comforting home cooking - Marinda Kook

html: Shepherd's pie is that  food that doesn't have much prestige. But it is honest food.  Food that doesn't ask questions and just settles gently in your stomach.

recipe.title: Shepherd's pie – simple, comforting home cooking
recipe.author: Marinda Engelbrecht

image.alt: Herderspastei - In sy eenvoud lekker huiskos

courses: Beef, Baking, Main course, Red meat

ingredientGroups.title: Filling
items: 500 ml  frozen vegetables of your choice
5 ml ground nutmeg (keep this for last – you sprinkle it on top of your pie)

steps: Boil the potatoes in water until soft.
Drain the water and add 60 ml butter to the potatoes. Mash it with a potato masher.
Warm the milk and stir it into the potato mixture. Check if you need more milk. It should be nice and soft. Salt and pepper to taste.
Set aside until your meat is done.
Fry the sliced onion and garlic in a pan in your butter until translucent.
Fry your mince until brown together with the onions.
Add the tin of tomatoes, Worcestershire sauce and mixed herbs to the meat mixture. Season with salt and pepper now and stir through. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add your frozen vegetables to the pan with the meat as well and let it simmer for another 5 minutes.
If you see your meat mixture is very watery, you can boil off the water or thicken it with Bisto.
Spoon your meat mixture into an oven dish.
Spoon your mashed potatoes on top of your meat and make patterns with the back of a fork.
Sprinkle ground nutmeg over the top layer of potato.
Bake at 180 degrees Celsius for 30 minutes.

notes: Enjoy the shepherd's pie with rice and a nice bit of gem squash. My family loved it on a slice of bread – what can I say?
You can fry some bacon with the onions for an extra treat. We like our food with a kick, so I mixed 10 ml chilli powder into our shepherd's pie.
